title: GitHub issue sync: pull state changes back from GitHub to canonical
description:
The current `syncTaskToGithubIssue()` in the dashboard pushes task state → GitHub (open/close issue, update title/body) but never reads back changes made in GitHub. If Marcus closes or adds labels to an issue in the GitHub UI, the canonical task file stays unchanged.

Fix: extend `syncTaskToGithubIssue()` in `rook-dashboard/src/lib/control/github-issues.ts` to also pull:
- `state` (open/closed) → if closed externally and canonical is not done, update `github_issue.state` and optionally notify
- `labels` → sync back to `task.labels` (additive, don't remove existing canonical labels)
- `assignees` → informational, write to a new `github_issue.assignees` field

Call direction: GET `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{number}` on every sync cycle (already called for `updateIssue` checks). The data is available; just needs to be written back to the canonical file after the API call.

Do NOT auto-transition the task to `done` on issue close — that's for ops-0041 (PR merge). Just update `github_issue.state` so the dashboard and dispatcher can see it.
